source_title: Supervised & Unsupervised land use and land cover classification using R || Random forest and CART

concepts.name: Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) Classification
concepts.definition: The process of categorizing earth's surface into classes based on physical characteristics (land cover) and human usage (land use) from remote sensing data.

concepts.name: Supervised Classification
concepts.definition: A machine learning approach where a model is trained on labeled data to predict class membership for unlabeled pixels.

concepts.name: Unsupervised Classification
concepts.definition: A clustering approach that groups pixels into spectral clusters without prior labels, often followed by manual interpretation.

concepts.name: Random Forest
concepts.definition: An ensemble learning method that builds multiple decision trees and aggregates their predictions to improve accuracy and reduce overfitting.

concepts.name: CART (Classification and Regression Trees)
concepts.definition: A binary decision tree algorithm that splits data recursively based on Gini impurity or variance reduction for classification and regression tasks.

procedures.name: Perform Supervised LULC Classification with Random Forest in R

steps.action: Install and load required R packages
steps.explanation: Packages like randomForest, raster, and caret are essential for processing imagery and modeling.
steps.gotcha: Package installation may fail due to missing system dependencies; ensure Rtools or similar is installed.

steps.explanation: Read raster data, extract spectral bands, and handle missing or no-data values to create a clean dataset.
steps.code_snippet: img <- brick('satellite_image.tif'); img <- calc(img, function(x) { x[x == -9999] <- NA; return(x) })
steps.gotcha: Large images can cause memory overflow; consider cropping or using raster::blockSize for processing.

steps.action: Prepare training data from vector samples
steps.explanation: Extract spectral values from training polygons (shapefiles) and create a data frame with features and class labels.
steps.code_snippet: train_shapes <- shapefile('training_samples.shp'); train_data <- data.frame(class=train_shapes$class, extract(img, train_shapes))
steps.gotcha: Training samples must be representative and balanced across classes to avoid bias.

steps.explanation: Fit the Random Forest classifier with tuning parameters like ntree (number of trees) and mtry (variables per split).
steps.code_snippet: set.seed(123); rf_model <- randomForest(class ~ ., data=train_data, ntree=500, mtry=sqrt(ncol(train_data)-1))
steps.gotcha: Too few trees may underfit; too many increase computation time without significant gains. Tune using caret::train.

steps.explanation: Predict on a held-out test set and compute accuracy metrics like overall accuracy, Kappa, and per-class statistics.
steps.gotcha: Overall accuracy can be misleading with imbalanced classes; always examine per-class metrics and confusion matrix.

steps.action: Apply model to entire image for classification
steps.explanation: Use the trained model to predict land cover classes for all pixels in the raster.
steps.code_snippet: lc_map <- predict(img, model=rf_model, filename='classified_output.tif', overwrite=TRUE, progress='text')
steps.gotcha: Prediction on large rasters may fail due to memory; use raster::writeRaster with datatype='INT1U' and format='GTiff' for efficiency.

procedures.name: Perform Unsupervised Classification using ISODATA in R

steps.explanation: Convert raster data to a matrix of spectral band values for all pixels or a sample.
steps.code_snippet: spectra <- getValuesBlock(img, row=1, nrows=nrow(img), col=1, ncols=ncol(img)); spectra <- na.omit(spectra)
steps.gotcha: Full-image extraction may exceed memory; use a random sample for large datasets.

steps.explanation: Apply k-means to group pixels into clusters based on spectral similarity.
steps.gotcha: Choosing the number of clusters (k) is arbitrary; use elbow method or silhouette score to inform decision.

steps.action: Assign clusters to land cover classes
steps.explanation: Manually interpret clusters using spectral plots or ancillary data (e.g., high-resolution imagery) to assign meaningful labels.
steps.code_snippet:
cluster_classes <- c(1,2,3); # Example: map cluster IDs to 'forest', 'water', 'urban'
class_raster <- reclassify(cluster_raster, rcl=cbind(1:10, cluster_classes))
steps.gotcha: Clusters may not correspond to actual land cover types; supervised methods are preferred if training data exists.

code_recipes.name: Random Forest Classification in R
code_recipes.purpose: Train a Random Forest model on spectral data to classify land cover, including model fitting and prediction.
code_recipes.code:
library(randomForest)
# Prepare data: train_data with 'class' factor column and numeric feature columns
set.seed(123)
model <- randomForest(class ~ ., data=train_data, ntree=500, importance=TRUE)
# Predict on new data
predictions <- predict(model, newdata=test_data)
# Evaluate
print(confusionMatrix(predictions, test_data$class))

code_recipes.name: CART Classification in R
code_recipes.purpose: Build and visualize a CART decision tree for land cover classification, including pruning.
code_recipes.code:
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
# Train CART model
model <- rpart(class ~ ., data=train_data, method='class', control=rpart.control(cp=0.01))
# Plot tree
rpart.plot(model, type=4, extra=101)
# Prune based on complexity parameter
opt_cp <- model$cptable[which.min(model$cptable[,'xerror']), 'CP']
pruned_model <- prune(model, cp=opt_cp)
# Predict
predictions <- predict(pruned_model, test_data, type='class')

gotchas.description: Overfitting in Random Forest due to excessive trees or noise
gotchas.context: When model accuracy on training data is high but test accuracy is low, often caused by too many trees or irrelevant features.
gotchas.solution: Tune mtry and ntree using out-of-bag error or cross-validation; use variable importance to select features; ensure adequate training samples.

gotchas.description: Class imbalance in training data leading to biased predictions
gotchas.context: Minority land cover classes (e.g., wetland) have few training samples, causing the model to ignore them.
gotchas.solution: Apply stratified sampling to balance classes; use class weights via randomForest(..., strata=class, sampsize=...); or employ oversampling (e.g., SMOTE) via caret::upSample.

gotchas.description: Memory errors when processing large satellite images
gotchas.context: High-resolution or large-area rasters exceed available RAM during extraction or prediction.
gotchas.solution: Use raster::writeRaster with blocks; sample pixels for unsupervised clustering; work with lower resolution or tile the image; increase memory limit via memory.limit() on Windows.

gotchas.description: Mismatch between unsupervised clusters and actual land cover classes
gotchas.context: Clusters from k-means or ISODATA may not align with known land cover types due to spectral variability or noise.
gotchas.solution: Combine unsupervised clustering with supervised labeling; use ancillary data (e.g., land masks) to refine; prefer supervised classification when label data is available.

gotchas.description: Incorrect factor levels in R leading to model errors
gotchas.context: Response variable not set as factor for classification, or factor levels mismatch between train and test data.
gotchas.solution: Ensure class column is factor: train_data$class <- as.factor(train_data$class); align levels using factor(train_data$class, levels=unique(c(train_data$class, test_data$class))).
